column1: # OWASP ZAP baseline rule tuning — REQ-SEC-008 (zap-baseline.yml).
# v1 scope: the baseline scans a PLACEHOLDER unauthenticated static-server target
# (`python -m http.server`) because no runnable application surface exists yet. That
# target legitimately lacks the security headers below, producing WARN-NEW noise with
# ZERO real findings (FAIL-NEW: 0). These 11 rules are IGNORED so the baseline gate is
# green on the placeholder while still failing on any genuine (non-ignored) finding.
# POST-v1 (when the real authenticated app is deployed): change the header rules
# (10020/10021/10038/10063/90004/90005) back to WARN/FAIL so missing security headers
# block the merge against the real surface.

column3: Anti-clickjacking (X-Frame-Options) — placeholder target; re-enable on real app
X-Content-Type-Options missing — placeholder target; re-enable on real app
Server version header — python http.server banner on the placeholder target
Content-Security-Policy not set — placeholder target; re-enable on real app
Permissions-Policy not set — placeholder target; re-enable on real app
Base64 disclosure — false positive on the static directory listing
Timestamp disclosure — directory-listing mtimes on the placeholder target
Source Code Disclosure (Perl) — false positive on the static directory listing
Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy missing — placeholder target; re-enable on real app
Sec-Fetch-Dest missing — request-header heuristic; re-enable on real app
